# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Router } from "https://deno.land/x/routing@v2.3.0/mod.ts";
import { serve, listenAndServe } from "https://deno.land/std@0.143.0/http/server.ts";

const app = new Router();

async function loadModule(modulePath: string): Promise<any> {
  try {
    const module = await import(modulePath);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Error loading module:", error);
  }
}

app.get('/', async ({ params, query }) => {
  let modulePaths = process.env.MODULE_PATHS || '';
  if (query.module_path && !modulePaths.includes(query.module_path)) {
    modulePaths += `,${query.module_path}`;
  }

  const modules: any[] = [];

  for (const path of modulePaths.split(',')) {
    const module = await loadModule(path);
    if (module) {
      modules.push(module.default || module);
    }
  }

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(modules));
});

listenAndServe({ port: 3000 }, () => {
  console.log("Server listening on port 3000");
});
